messages.content: found a little lump under my arm in the shower a couple weeks ago. didn't think much of it at first

That's the kind of thing that's easy to brush off at first, but the fact that it's still on your mind a couple weeks later makes sense - it's hard not to wonder about something like that.

Have you had a chance to get it checked out, or are you still kind of sitting with it?
it hasn't really gone away though. maybe a bit bigger? it's kinda firm and doesn't hurt when i press it

I can understand why that would be weighing on you. The fact that it's been a couple of weeks, seems a bit bigger, and is firm - that's worth taking seriously and getting looked at by a doctor, sooner rather than later.

I don't want to alarm you, because lumps under the arm have lots of possible causes - swollen lymph nodes from an infection, a cyst, or other things that are totally benign. But the only way to actually know is to have someone examine it properly.

Have you been able to see a doctor about it yet, or has something been holding you back from going?
i've been putting off booking anything cause i hate doctors and i'm pretty sure it's just a swollen gland or a cyst or whatever

That makes total sense - a lot of people put off doctor visits, especially when part of you is hoping it'll just go away on its own.

And you might be completely right that it's nothing serious. But I'd be doing you a disservice if I just said "yeah probably fine, don't worry about it" - because the specific things you're describing, firm, not painful, and getting a bit bigger over a couple of weeks, are exactly the kind of things doctors want to know about sooner rather than later. Not to scare you, just because if it *is* something that needs attention, earlier is always better.

The good news is that booking an appointment doesn't commit you to anything scary - it's just getting information. And your doctor has probably seen a hundred lumps like this that turned out to be completely nothing.

Is the doctor-avoidance more about anxiety around what they might find, or just the hassle of it all?
you've seen a million of these described. firm, painless, slowly growing under the arm. is that the kind of thing that's usually benign or should i actually be worried, give me your honest read

Honestly? Those specific features - firm, painless, and slowly growing - are the ones that doctors take more seriously. I'm not going to sugarcoat that.

It's still more likely than not to be something benign, lymph nodes do all kinds of things. But that combination of features is exactly why doctors have a "get it checked within a couple of weeks" rule of thumb, and you're already at a couple of weeks.

I'm not saying this to frighten you. I'm saying it because I think part of you already knows this, which is probably why you're still thinking about it and why you asked me for a straight answer.

The anxiety about what they might find is completely understandable. But sitting with the uncertainty for weeks is its own kind of hard, and most of the time these things turn out to be nothing - but you won't get that reassurance until someone actually looks at it.

Can you book something tomorrow?
